title: trifolium campestre var. subsessile
intro: Trifolium campestre var. subsessile, also known as the subsessile or creeping white clover, is a perennial herb belonging to the Fabaceae family. It is widely used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Trifolium campestre var. subsessile include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, it is often used as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create topical ointments and salves for skin issues.
